slices.name: validate_manifest_headers
slices.code:
16 | def validate_manifest_headers(headers: Optional[Iterable[str]]) -> None:
  17 |     if headers is None:
  18 |         raise ValueError("Manifest CSV is missing headers")
  19 |     missing = [col for col in MANIFEST_COLUMNS if col not in headers]
  20 |     if missing:
  21 |         raise ValueError(f"Manifest CSV missing required columns: {', '.join(missing)}")

slices.name: load_manifest_csv
slices.code:
24 | def load_manifest_csv(path: Path) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
  25 |     path = Path(path)
  26 |     with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", newline="") as handle:
  27 |         reader = csv.DictReader(handle)
  28 |         validate_manifest_headers(reader.fieldnames)
  29 |         rows: List[Dict[str, str]] = [row for row in reader]
  30 |     return rows

slices.name: is_suspicious_manifest_path
slices.code:
50 | def is_suspicious_manifest_path(path: str, suspicious_directories: List[str] = None) -> bool:
  51 |     if suspicious_directories is None:
  52 |         suspicious_directories = DEFAULT_SUSPICIOUS_DIRECTORIES
  53 |     normalized = _normalize_path(path)
  54 |     return any(f"/{entry.lower()}/" in normalized or normalized.endswith(f"/{entry.lower()}") for entry in suspicious_directories)

slices.name: analyze_manifest_rows
slices.code:
57 | def analyze_manifest_rows(rows: List[Dict[str, str]], suspicious_directories: List[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
  58 |     if suspicious_directories is None:
  59 |         suspicious_directories = DEFAULT_SUSPICIOUS_DIRECTORIES
  60 | 
  61 |     abs_seen: set[str] = set()
  62 |     rel_seen: set[str] = set()
  63 |     duplicate_abs_paths: List[str] = []
  64 |     duplicate_rel_paths: List[str] = []
  65 |     missing_paths: List[str] = []
  66 |     suspicious_paths: List[str] = []
  67 |     root_paths: set[str] = set()
  68 | 
  69 |     for row in rows:
  70 |         abs_path = row.get("abs_path", "").strip()
  71 |         rel_path = row.get("rel_path", "").strip()
  72 |         root = row.get("root", "").strip()
  73 | 
  74 |         root_paths.add(root)
  75 | 
  76 |         if abs_path in abs_seen:
  77 |             duplicate_abs_paths.append(abs_path)
  78 |         else:
  79 |             abs_seen.add(abs_path)
  80 | 
  81 |         if rel_path in rel_seen:
  82 |             duplicate_rel_paths.append(rel_path)
  83 |         else:
  84 |             rel_seen.add(rel_path)
  85 | 
  86 |         if abs_path:
  87 |             if not Path(abs_path).exists():
  88 |                 missing_paths.append(abs_path)
  89 |             if is_suspicious_manifest_path(abs_path, suspicious_directories):
  90 |                 suspicious_paths.append(abs_path)
  91 | 
  92 |     status = "PASS"
  93 |     if missing_paths or duplicate_abs_paths or duplicate_rel_paths:
  94 |         status = "FAIL"
  95 |     elif suspicious_paths:
  96 |         status = "WARN"
  97 | 
  98 |     return {
  99 |         "status": status,
 100 |         "summary": {
 101 |             "row_count": len(rows),
 102 |             "root_count": len(root_paths),
 103 |             "missing_files": len(missing_paths),
 104 |             "duplicate_abs_paths": len(duplicate_abs_paths),
 105 |             "duplicate_rel_paths": len(duplicate_rel_paths),
 106 |             "suspicious_paths": len(suspicious_paths),
 107 |         },
 108 |         "details": {
 109 |             "missing_files": missing_paths,
 110 |             "duplicate_abs_paths": duplicate_abs_paths,
 111 |             "duplicate_rel_paths": duplicate_rel_paths,
 112 |             "suspicious_paths": suspicious_paths,
 113 |         },
 114 |     }

slices.name: manifest_row_count
slices.code:
117 | def manifest_row_count(path: Path) -> int:
 118 |     rows = load_manifest_csv(path)
 119 |     return len(rows)
